preamble: The shift from the voiced dental fricative [ð] to the voiceless labiodental fricative [f] involves both fronting and devoicing. This typically occurs in child language development where labial sounds are substituted for more difficult dental ones, or in dialects where the distinction between voiced and voiceless dental fricatives is neutralized in favor of labiodental outcomes.

examples.note: Developmental substitution: fronting and devoicing the dental fricative.
Neutralization of the voicing contrast in final positions, resulting in TH-fronting to [f].
